Predicate argument has wrong type:
  atLocation pump_bottle toilet_tank: pump_bottle is not a agent
attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put the pump bottle in the garbage can.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene is a bathroom environment containing several objects relevant to the robotic planning task. The primary objects of interest are the "pump bottle" and the "garbage can".

**Objects and Their Types:**

- **Pump Bottle**: This is an object (not a receptacle) with a distinct pump dispenser top. It is purple in color and is currently resting on the white toilet tank. It is not held by the agent and is not inside any receptacle.
- **Garbage Can**: This is a receptacle, colored dark green. It is positioned on the floor, directly beneath the bathroom vanity countertop. The garbage can is open and empty. It is not currently being interacted with by the agent.

**Quantities:**

- There is one pump bottle.
- There is one garbage can (receptacle).

**Spatial Relationships:**

- The pump bottle is located on top of the toilet tank.
- The garbage can is on the floor, under the vanity.
- The agent is not currently at the location of either the pump bottle or the garbage can.
- The pump bottle is not in any receptacle.
- The garbage can is openable (implied by its design as a trash bin) and is currently in an open state (no lid is visible or closed).

**Current State of Objects:**

- The pump bottle is stationary, not held, and not in any receptacle.
- The garbage can is open and empty.
- The agent has not yet performed any actions (e.g., not at location, not holding anything).

**Visual Properties:**

- The pump bottle is purple with a white pump head and is placed next to a roll of toilet paper and a spray bottle on the toilet tank.
- The garbage can is dark green, rectangular, and sits on a tiled floor. A small pink object (possibly a bar of soap) is visible on the vanity countertop above it, but it is not relevant to the task.
- The toilet tank is white and serves as a surface for the pump bottle and other items.
- The bathroom has light-colored tiled walls and floor, with a dark green vanity countertop.

The task requires the agent to move to the pump bottle, pick it up, move to the garbage can, and place the pump bottle inside it. The garbage can is accessible and ready to receive the object.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_pump_bottle_in_garbage_can)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        pump_bottle - object
        garbage_can - receptacle
        toilet_tank - object  ; not a receptacle, but a surface where the pump bottle is located
    )
    (:init
        ; The agent is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation agent1 pump_bottle))
        (not (atLocation agent1 garbage_can))
        (not (atLocation agent1 toilet_tank))

        ; The pump bottle is on the toilet tank
        (atLocation pump_bottle toilet_tank)

        ; The garbage can is on the floor and is openable (and currently open, as per scene)
        (openable garbage_can)
        (opened garbage_can)

        ; The garbage can is empty
        (not (inReceptacle pump_bottle garbage_can))

        ; The agent is not holding anything
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle pump_bottle garbage_can)
        )
    )
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem put_pump_bottle_in_garbage_can)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        pump_bottle - object
        garbage_can - receptacle
        toilet_tank - object  ; not a receptacle, but a surface where the pump bottle is located
    )
    (:init
        ; The agent is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation agent1 pump_bottle))
        (not (atLocation agent1 garbage_can))
        (not (atLocation agent1 toilet_tank))

        ; The pump bottle is on the toilet tank
        (atLocation pump_bottle toilet_tank)

        ; The garbage can is on the floor and is openable (and currently open, as per scene)
        (openable garbage_can)
        (opened garbage_can)

        ; The garbage can is empty
        (not (inReceptacle pump_bottle garbage_can))

        ; The agent is not holding anything
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle pump_bottle garbage_can)
        )
    )
)

    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put the pump bottle in the garbage can.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene is a bathroom environment with several objects relevant to the planning task. The key objects and their properties are as follows:

1. Objects and their types:
   - The “pump bottle” is an object (not a receptacle) and is visually identifiable as a small, dark green bottle with a pump dispenser, sitting on top of the toilet tank. It is not currently held by any agent.
   - The “garbage can” is a receptacle, colored dark green, with an open top, positioned on the floor to the left of the toilet and beneath the countertop. It is not openable (no lid or mechanism to open/close), so it does not have the (openable) predicate.
   - The toilet tank is a large, white, rectangular object that serves as a surface for holding items. It is not a receptacle in the domain sense (it cannot hold objects inside it), so it is classified as an object.
   - The countertop is a dark green surface extending over the sink area. It is not a receptacle and is classified as an object.
   - The sink is a receptacle, located in the countertop, with a visible basin. It is not openable.

2. Quantities:
   - There is exactly one pump bottle.
   - There is exactly one garbage can.
   - There is exactly one toilet tank.
   - There is exactly one countertop.
   - There is exactly one sink.

3. Spatial relationships:
   - The pump bottle is currently on top of the toilet tank.
   - The garbage can is on the floor, adjacent to the toilet and beneath the countertop.
   - The sink is embedded in the countertop.
   - The toilet tank is adjacent to the countertop and sink area.

4. Current state of objects relevant to the task:
   - The pump bottle is not held by any agent and is not inside any receptacle.
   - The garbage can is empty and ready to receive objects.
   - The pump bottle is not cleaned, heated, cooled, sliced, or toggled.
   - The agent is not currently at any object’s location (initial state).

5. Visual properties for object distinction:
   - The pump bottle is small, dark green, and has a black pump top. It is placed on the white toilet tank.
   - The garbage can is dark green, rectangular with an open top, and has a slightly raised rim. It is on the tiled floor.
   - The toilet tank is white and rectangular, with a flush handle visible on the front.
   - The countertop is dark green and flat, extending over the sink.
   - The sink is integrated into the countertop and has a visible basin.

The task requires the agent to pick up the pump bottle from the toilet tank and place it into the garbage can. The garbage can is accessible and does not need to be opened. The agent must first navigate to the toilet tank to pick up the pump bottle, then move to the garbage can to deposit it.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_pump_bottle_in_garbage_can)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        pump_bottle - object
        garbage_can - receptacle
        toilet_tank - object
        countertop - object
        sink - receptacle
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation agent1 toilet_tank) ; agent starts near the toilet tank where the pump bottle is
        (inReceptacle pump_bottle toilet_tank) ; pump bottle is on the toilet tank (treated as surface/object)
        (not (holdsAny agent1)) ; agent is not holding anything
        (not (opened garbage_can)) ; garbage can is not openable, so opened is irrelevant, but we can omit or leave false
        (not (isClean pump_bottle))
        (not (isHot pump_bottle))
        (not (isCool pump_bottle))
        (not (isSliced pump_bottle))
        (not (isToggled pump_bottle))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle pump_bottle garbage_can)
    ))
)
```

        (inReceptacle pump_bottle toilet_tank) ; pump bottle is on the toilet tank (treated as surface/object)
        (not (holdsAny agent1)) ; agent is not holding anything
        (not (opened garbage_can)) ; garbage can is not openable, so opened is irrelevant, but we can omit or leave false
        (not (isClean pump_bottle))
        (not (isHot pump_bottle))
        (not (isCool pump_bottle))
        (not (isSliced pump_bottle))
        (not (isToggled pump_bottle))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle pump_bottle garbage_can)
    ))
)
attempts.plan:
(gotolocation agent1 pump_bottle)
(pickupobject agent1 pump_bottle)
(gotolocation agent1 garbage_can)
(putobjectinreceptacle agent1 pump_bottle garbage_can)

(define (problem put_pump_bottle_in_garbage_can)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        pump_bottle - object
        garbage_can - receptacle
        toilet_tank - object
        countertop - object
        sink - receptacle
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation agent1 toilet_tank) ; agent starts near the toilet tank where the pump bottle is
        (inReceptacle pump_bottle toilet_tank) ; pump bottle is on the toilet tank (treated as surface/object)
        (not (holdsAny agent1)) ; agent is not holding anything
        (not (opened garbage_can)) ; garbage can is not openable, so opened is irrelevant, but we can omit or leave false
        (not (isClean pump_bottle))
        (not (isHot pump_bottle))
        (not (isCool pump_bottle))
        (not (isSliced pump_bottle))
        (not (isToggled pump_bottle))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle pump_bottle garbage_can)
    ))
)
